verdict.note: configured (gugu live :5173): .si-launcher-select shows [codex·codex, reclaude·claude], .si-agent-picker absent; fallback (isolated no-launcher board): dropdown absent, harness radios present (count 1)

Real browser: /api/launchers returns {launchers(name-sorted: aaa-first,claude-glm,reclaude), default:reclaude}; dropdown initial value = reclaude (the config default), NOT aaa-first (alphabetically first); harness radios absent. Precedence confirmed: remembered valid pick (aaa-first) wins; invalid remembered → falls to reclaude.

Real browser: session launched under claude-glm; /api/board carries launcher:claude-glm; both the console list row AND the map-side SessionWindow render a .sess-launcher badge = harness glyph + 'claude-glm' (title 'launcher: claude-glm').

Real browser (vite dev → live :8787), Playwright route-injected launcher='claude-glm'/harness='claude' onto a session (the exact data that WOULD have drawn the old badge). DOM read: .sess-launcher=0, .sess-launcher-name=0, .sess-launcher .si-agent-glyph=0, across 6 rendered rows; the launcher field still rides /api/board. Board reads clean, no badge.

Resume-launcher-pin (tooth 4): launcherCmd() returns the record's pinned launch_cmd verbatim even with SPEXCODE_CLAUDE_CMD flipped to a different default — so resume replays the ORIGINAL launcher (its config-dir env), not the drifted default. newSession pins h.baseCmd(chosen?.cmd) at creation (named + unnamed). Proven via unit tests (launcherCmd pinned-wins; claude/codex baseCmd resolution).

Real backend, real restart onto a different default. Session N created --launcher lA (cmd carries CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=cfgA), session U created unnamed under ambient SPEXCODE_CLAUDE_CMD (cfgU); both records pinned launch_cmd at creation. Then: POST exit both, EDIT lA.cmd -> cfgB in spexcode.json AND restart the backend with SPEXCODE_CLAUDE_CMD -> cfgB (the mass-restore condition). POST resume: both 200; regenerated launch.sh replays the EXACT pinned command — N under cfgA (immune even to its own launcher's edited config), U under cfgU — neither rewritten to cfgB; both carry --resume <session-id> and came back online. The unnamed zero-config session is pinned too (launch_cmd frozen at birth).

Real dashboard at http://localhost:5179/#/sessions/new via Playwright: .si-launcher-select count=1, .si-agent-picker count=0, options claude/claude-glm/codex/reclaude, selected default claude-glm; screenshot attached.

configured project (4 launchers): .si-launcher-select present with exactly one option per /api/launchers profile (claude/claude-glm/codex/reclaude), .si-agent-picker ABSENT, 0 radio inputs. Zero-config project (fresh spex init, :8951): same select renders with built-in claude/codex, no radios (launcher-zeroconf.png). The one .si-agent-glyph on the page sits in the composer's si-launcher-harness adornment, not on any session row.

GET /api/launchers = {launchers:[claude,claude-glm,codex,reclaude], default:claude-glm}. Fresh browser (si.launcher cleared): dropdown pre-selects claude-glm — the configured default, NOT alphabetically-first claude. Remembered valid pick (codex) wins on reload; invalid remembered (ghost-launcher) falls back to claude-glm.

board payload carries launcher on every session (reclaude/codex/claude-glm) yet document.querySelectorAll('.sess-launcher').length === 0 and no .sess-launcher-name on any row across 6 rows (map window + console list) — badge gone, data kept and API-exposed.

named session under cfgA (wrapper pinning CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) + unnamed default session (claude-glm); both exited, backend restarted with SPEXCODE_CLAUDE_CMD=launchB.sh; reopen regenerated launch.sh replaying launcher A's exact wrapper for the named session and the frozen original claude-glm cmd for the unnamed one — neither rewritten to B; --resume present so the conversation is found in the original config dir.

Missing sessions.defaultLauncher now reports an actionable config error through /api/launchers, API create, CLI new, and dashboard DOM; no built-in claude fallback and no worktree/POST on no-choice create.

note: Retracting because the screenshot evidence captured the graph view and did not visibly show the New Session launcher error prompt; DOM/API readings were correct but frontend evidence was too weak.

verdict.note: Visible Sessions board shows the missing-default error under the launcher picker, select stays unset, no harness radios render, and Enter submits no POST; CLI/API also reject no-choice create without creating a worktree.

note: Retracting because the visible screenshot used the unclear disabled placeholder text 'set default', which looked like a launcher choice instead of a missing configuration state.

verdict.note: Visible Sessions board now labels the disabled launcher placeholder 'no default configured', shows the missing-default error, leaves the select unset, and submits no POST; no built-in claude fallback.

note: Retracting because this reading measured a dashboard placeholder/error UI that was overfit; missing-default fail-loud is only for create paths that can omit launcher (CLI/API/@new).

verdict.note: Real temp project on backend :19963: no sessions.defaultLauncher made CLI new, POST /api/sessions, and @new fail loud with the actionable config error and create no worktree/session; after defaultLauncher=fake, bare spex new and @new created sessions with launcher=fake; explicit --launcher fake also created launcher=fake.

Re-measured after /api/launchers→/api/settings absorption: real browser on the worktree dashboard against a live backend — dropdown lists claude-glm/codex/reclaude from GET /api/settings and pre-selects claude-glm, the configured sessions.defaultLauncher, not the alphabetical first.

Real dashboard New box (vite from this worktree :9010 → backend :9001): .si-launcher-select present with exactly one option per profile (codex, reclaude); .si-agent-picker harness radiogroup ABSENT; GET /api/settings returns [{codex,codex},{reclaude,claude}] default reclaude — the dropdown renders exactly that list. Fresh-init default (claude/codex) launchers are ordinary config entries.

Real dashboard, launchers {codex, reclaude} with defaultLauncher=reclaude (NOT alpha-first codex). Fresh browser context (no si.launcher) → dropdown pre-selects reclaude, agreeing with CLI default; GET /api/settings reports {launchers:[codex,reclaude], default:reclaude}. With localStorage si.launcher=codex remembered, reload → codex wins over the default. Order-of-precedence honored.

Config exposes launchers {reclaude,codex} but NO sessions.defaultLauncher. All three no-choice create surfaces refuse identically with HTTP 400 + actionable message ('sessions.defaultLauncher is required ... set it in spexcode.json/spexcode.local.json'): (A) spex new no --launcher, (B) POST /api/sessions no launcher, (C) @new dispatch. No session/worktree created (session ls empty). No silent built-in 'claude' fallback. Explicit --launcher is validated against the configured list ('unknown launcher no-such-launcher (configured: codex, reclaude)'), proving --launcher bypasses the default requirement by a visible named choice.

W1 pinned launch_cmd=reclaude at creation. Changed config (reclaude cmd += --MARKER-B-NEW-CONFIG, default→codex), stopped+resumed W1: regenerated launch.sh replays the ORIGINAL pinned 'reclaude --dangerously-skip-permissions --resume <id>' — NO marker, NOT codex; record.launch_cmd frozen. The relaunch replays launcher A's exact command from the record, never re-resolved to the current default B or edited config, so --resume finds the conversation in A's config dir. (Named-launcher case; the record-frozen launch_cmd mechanism is identical for an unnamed/default session.)

Re-measured at HEAD 9f418a66: with launchers configured but NO defaultLauncher, all three no-choice surfaces refuse with the actionable 400 (spex session new; POST /api/sessions; @new dispatch via a local-issue mention reports new→failed with the same message), no session/worktree created, no silent built-in fallback; explicit --launcher is validated by name ('unknown launcher … (configured: codex, reclaude, stub)'); restoring the default makes no-choice create use that profile.

Re-measured at HEAD 9f418a66: W1 pinned launchCmd under launcher A at creation; after editing A's cmd (+--MARKER-B-NEW-CONFIG) and flipping defaultLauncher to B, stop+resume regenerates launch.sh exec'ing the ORIGINAL pinned cmd + --resume <id> — 0 marker hits, not B; record.launchCmd frozen; worker back online. (Every create now resolves to a named launcher — no unnamed/built-in path exists; the record-frozen launchCmd is the same mechanism prior readings noted.)
